Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: total = slot price x convives, not sum of line prices

Root cause: DB trigger recalculate_order_total() was computing
SUM(unit_price * quantity) across ALL order_lines. With 4 categories
per convive, this inflated the total by 4x.

Fix: trigger now uses DISTINCT ON (meal_slot_id, guest_name) with
menu_unit_price — correctly computes slot_price × nb_convives.
Also backfills all existing orders with correct totals.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add progress feedback and local SSD staging to Cell 20 preprocessing

Cell 20 (piper_train.preprocess) went silent for 20-60 min on Colab.
Now streams subprocess output in real time and monitors .pt file creation
every 10s to show concrete progress. Also stages data to local /content/
SSD before preprocessing to avoid Google Drive FUSE latency (~50-200ms
per file op), and bumps --max-workers from 2 to 4. CLAUDE.md updated
with transcription cache docs from prior session.

commit.message:
Fix math and improve analysis: Gaussian/Poisson fits with reduced chi-square

- Fix difference_percent to divide by sqrt(mu) not mu
- Rename gausian -> gaussian, noices -> noises
- Add reduced chi-square (chi2/dof) to all histogram plots
- Switch to Freedman-Diaconis binning across all histogram cells
- Handle dof=0 gracefully (show N/A instead of inf)
- Add inverse-square-law fit with error bars to distance vs counts plot
- Add chi-square goodness-of-fit explanation markdown cell

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Stripe checkout flow end-to-end

Root causes fixed:
1. Server Action redirect to external Stripe URL unreliable — replaced
   with client-side fetch('/api/checkout') + window.location.href
2. try/catch in startCheckout swallowed NEXT_REDIRECT errors — add
   explicit re-throw guard for digest.startsWith('NEXT_REDIRECT')
3. Landing page pricing CTAs were plain <Link href='/signup'> — wired
   to CheckoutButton with correct bundleId/mode per plan
4. Auth redirect lost checkout intent — unauthenticated users now
   redirected to /login?redirectTo=%2Fdashboard%23pricing so they
   land back at the pricing section after signing in

CheckoutButton.tsx:
- Pure fetch-based client component, no Server Action dep
- 401 → redirect to /login with pricing section anchor preserved
- Inline error message below button on API failure
- Keeps spinner until window.location.href fires (no flicker)
- Accepts className and label props for reuse on landing page

app/page.tsx:
- PRICING_PLANS gains bundleId + mode fields
- CheckoutButton replaces <Link> CTA on all three pricing cards

app/dashboard/page.tsx:
- Drop startCheckout import; CheckoutButton needs no action prop

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(kernel): surface HTTP client configuration errors instead of silencing them (#230)

Previously, `new_with_http_config` constructors on all API clients used
`.unwrap_or_else(|e| { warn!(...); Client::new() })`, silently falling
back to an unconfigured HTTP client when proxy or timeout setup failed.
This made misconfigured proxy URLs or invalid TLS settings invisible.

Changes:
- `HttpClientConfig::from_cli_and_env` and `from_cli_and_env_full` now
  return `anyhow::Result<Self>` and validate proxy URLs eagerly via
  `url::Url::parse()` before any HTTP request is made
- Added `proxy_url: Option<String>` field to `HttpClientConfig`; when
  set, `create_client()` configures `reqwest::Proxy::all()` and returns
  an error on invalid URLs
- Added `--proxy <URL>` CLI flag (env: `RAPS_PROXY`) globally on the
  `raps` CLI so users can route all requests through a corporate proxy
- All `new_with_http_config` constructors across raps-kernel, raps-oss,
  raps-dm, raps-da, raps-derivative, raps-reality, raps-webhooks, and
  all raps-acc sub-clients now return `anyhow::Result<Self>`
- `new()` constructors (default config, no proxy) use `.expect()` since
  the default configuration is always valid
- CLI entry points propagate the error with `?`; MCP factory closures
  (which run after config validation) use `.expect()` with a clear message
- Fixed two pre-existing bugs in `raps-cli`: duplicate `glob.workspace`
  key in Cargo.toml, and corrupted brace structure in pipeline.rs that
  prevented compilation

Closes #154

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
docs: explain why this can't be App Store safe

Added detailed "Why this can't be App Store safe" section documenting
all 5 public API approaches we investigated and why each fails:

- NSUserDefaults: WKWebView's empty m_identifier skips the read
- Info.plist: CADisableMinimumFrameDuration* has no effect on WKWebView
- WKWebViewConfiguration: no public frame rate API exists
- CADisplayLink: can't reach WebKit's internal compositor
- KVC backdoors: Apple is actively closing them

Apple provides no public API. The private _features API is the only
runtime toggle, same as Safari uses internally. Clearly documented
who should and shouldn't use this plugin.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: benchmark CI hang — skip regular tests and cap timeout

- Add -run=^$ to bench job so only Benchmark* functions run (not
  integration tests which were hanging on stdin pipe reads)
- Add -timeout=15m flag so go test enforces a per-package deadline
- Add timeout-minutes: 20 at the job level as a hard ceiling
- Fix octree reduce: nil out merged children so they cannot be
  double-collected by subsequent collectReducible traversals

Root cause: go test -bench=. without -run=^$ runs ALL tests in every
package including internal/cli which builds the binary via TestMain
and executes CLI integration tests. With -count=3 and no timeout
the job stalled indefinitely.

consulting-quality branded reports with citations and charts.

commit.message:
fix(mic): retune YIN for real instrument detection, fix audio session conflicts

Mic detection was tuned for speaker-to-mic echo (phone speaker → mic), which
is wrong for actual piano/keyboard input. Three key changes:

1. PitchDetector: Re-enable octave correction (essential for piano — low notes
   have stronger harmonics than fundamental). Raise minConfidence 0.30→0.55 to
   reject noise. Tighten YIN threshold 0.25→0.15 for fewer false positives.
   Raise rmsThreshold 0.008→0.012 for better silence gating. Add diagnostic
   logging for first 10 voiced detections.

2. SoundManager: Remove Audio.setAudioModeAsync() from preload() — it was
   clobbering the mic's PlayAndRecord session back to Playback mode.

3. ExpoAudioEngine: Add polyphony volume scaling (1/sqrt(n)) to the
   createAndPlaySound() fallback path to prevent clipping.

4. InputManager: Fix dispose() to explicitly stop mic recording before cleanup.

Add PWA foundation: manifest, service worker, icons (#2870)

* Add PWA foundation: manifest, service worker, icons

- Add web app manifest at /manifest.json with name, icons, theme color,
  and screenshots for richer install UI
- Add service worker at /service-worker.js that pre-caches an offline
  fallback page and serves it when navigation requests fail
- Add /offline page shown when the user has no network connection
- Generate real app icons (192, 512, maskable) from the TV icon SVG
- Fix apple-touch-icon.png which was previously an empty file
- Add PWA meta tags to the layout: manifest link, theme-color,
  apple-mobile-web-app-* tags
- Update viewport to include viewport-fit=cover for notch support
- Add safe-area-inset padding for sticky nav in standalone mode

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Rename PwaController to ProgressiveWebAppController

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Replace controller-served manifest/SW with plain static files

The manifest and service worker had no dynamic content, so there's no
reason to route them through Rails. Moving both to public/ means they're
served directly by the web server with no overhead.

Removes the ProgressiveWebAppController and its views entirely.
Adds system tests for the manifest and offline routes.

commit.message:
Add MK LV3-4 4-Pointer Part 1 blog posts (Week 1 & Week 2)

Creates 4 new teaching pages covering strategies from the MK 4-Pointers
LV3-4 Part 1 workbook, clearly named with '4pt' to distinguish them from
the 5-pointer series:

- Ordering & Sequences: line simulation, arrow-flipping cycles (mod 6),
  balance grouping (parity check), ruler marking (C(4,2) distances)
- Equal & Balance: symbol scale equations, pairwise sums formula
- Fun Math: magic square error detection, parallel scheduling, necklace
  matching, concentration comparison, minimum coin moves; includes Week 1
  Review (Day 5) problems
- Basic Word Problems: mirror conservation pattern for 2- and 3-container
  "take as many as remain" problems

Also updates teaching/index.md with two new 4-Pointer sections.

commit.message:
Calculate start dates before scheduling, feed them into scheduler

Step 1: compute effective start date per type (today + delay).
Step 2: for each month, determine fromDay per type and pass to
scheduleMonth, which distributes items only within available days.
No post-hoc date shifting — the scheduler works with correct dates
from the start.

Add LangChain and LlamaIndex integration middleware

- LangChain: SentinelCallbackHandler scans LLM inputs/outputs via
  on_llm_start, on_chat_model_start, on_llm_end callbacks
- LlamaIndex: SentinelEventHandler scans queries/responses via
  on_event_start/on_event_end with support for query, llm event types
- Both support raise_on_block mode, manual scan APIs, and factory
  functions with configurable block thresholds
- Zero dependency on langchain/llamaindex in core — duck-typed interfaces
- 43 new tests (22 LangChain, 21 LlamaIndex), 137 total passing
- Example apps demonstrating both integrations
- Optional deps: sentinel-ai[langchain], sentinel-ai[llamaindex]
